title: Tri-View Ego World + IDM Foundation Model
description: Two-stage ego-view world model + Image-Layered IDM/control stack
summary: Tri-View is being rewritten as a two-stage Ego World + IDM foundation model. Stage 1 learns an interactive UMI Ego World: wrist cameras and fixed or floating robot-head cameras are all ego views, all views share overlapping context, and future video responds to real-time external action conditions like a teleoperation video model. Stage 2 explains how to use those generated videos through Image-Layered decomposition: the robot layer trains IDM and supports pose / VO, the object-interaction layer trains process reward and RL self-supervision, and the scene layer enables physics-aware augmentation or self-adaptation. The project answers three questions: how should the world change, what interaction-centric information is needed, and how to decode visual futures into robot position, velocity, and current-loop commands.
asset_alt: UMI dataset annotation and subtask segmentation visualization panel
asset_caption: Dataset QA: synchronized wrist views, 3D pose, EEF traces, current segment, and action log after subtask splitting.
details: Ego-view definition: wrist cameras and fixed or floating robot-head cameras are all treated as ego because they are robot-attached or robot-centered observation streams. Tri-View no longer means only a missing external fixed camera; it means synchronized ego views that share scene context and partially overlapping POVs.

Stage 1, Ego World: train an action-conditioned multi-view video world model that accepts current ego observations, shared context, and real-time external action conditions, then predicts action-responsive futures across wrist and robot-head views. This is the interactive UMI World Model side of the project.

Stage 2, IDM base: use the generated ego rollouts to infer executable robot actions. The IDM maps visual transitions and layer states into commands, then a controller-specific decoder chooses position-loop targets, velocity-loop commands, or current/torque-loop supervision depending on the required interaction.

Object-interaction-robot framing: the representation is not generic layered image editing. It separates robot motion, object interaction state, and scene/background so the model can reason about what changes, what caused it, and which part is useful for control.

Robot layer: when the arm is visible, use the robot layer in head / third ego view for robot pose or joint estimation and IDM supervision; this can also regularize wrist-view visual odometry by providing a robot-motion prior.

No-arm-visible branch: when the robot is not visible in a wrist or head view, use VO / SLAM / feed-forward visual geometry to recover camera egomotion and local scene coordinates, then let IDM infer action from object and scene transitions rather than direct arm pixels.

Object-interaction layer: treat object state, contact, and 6-DoF trajectory as the central dynamics target. ObjectForesight is the closest object-layer reference: it models future object motion with mesh/pose trajectories rather than raw pixels.

Scene layer: use physically and task-aware scene/background transformations, following the RoboEngine direction, for augmentation, domain adaptation, and self-adaptation without changing the robot/object interaction evidence.

Data bottleneck: the hard part is building usable layered and ego-view data. The current four TODOs are now aligned to this: Cosmos3 testing, three-view/ego-view dataset replacement, Gamma-World reproduction, and segment+inpaint/SVG/Image-Layered dataset preparation.

Positioning: Gamma-World remains the multi-agent interactive world-model baseline; Qwen-Image-Layered remains the layer decomposition baseline; ObjectForesight motivates the object-interaction layer; EVA-style IDM rewards motivate converting generated videos into executable control evidence.

references.title: Gamma-World: Generative Multi-Agent World Modeling Beyond Two Players
references.notes: MAIN REFERENCE / collision baseline. Gamma-World defines the generic synchronized action-conditioned multi-agent world-model lane, with Simplex Rotary Agent Encoding, Sparse Hub Attention, and causal real-time rollout. Tri-View should reproduce or baseline against this direction, then pivot the claim toward view/layer/eval utility for manipulation rather than agent-count scaling.

references.title: Qwen-Image-Layered: Layered Decomposition for Inherent Editability
references.notes: MAIN REFERENCE / layer-decomposition route. Qwen-Image-Layered decomposes a single image into multiple RGBA layers for independent editing and recomposition. Use it as the direct image-layer baseline and as evidence that robot manipulation needs stronger object/contact and robot/ego supervision than generic editable layers.

references.title: ObjectForesight: Predicting Future 3D Object Trajectories from Human Videos
references.notes: MAIN REFERENCE / object-centric dynamics route. ObjectForesight predicts future 6-DoF object trajectories from short egocentric video histories and builds large-scale pseudo-ground-truth object trajectory data from human videos using segmentation, tracking, reconstruction, depth/camera motion, and pose estimation. Use as the reference for object/contact layer targets and for the segment+track+reconstruct data pipeline.

references.title: Walk through Paintings: Egocentric World Models from Internet Priors
references.notes: Support reference for Ego World: transforms pretrained video diffusion models into action-conditioned egocentric world models with motor-command conditioning and structural consistency evaluation.

references.title: LOME: Learning Human-Object Manipulation with Action-Conditioned Egocentric World Model
references.notes: Support reference for action-conditioned egocentric human-object interaction video: uses reference image, text prompt, and per-frame actions to generate physically plausible manipulation videos.

references.title: EgoSim: Egocentric World Simulator for Embodied Interaction Generation
references.notes: Support reference for continuously updated egocentric interaction simulation with 3D state updating, geometry-action-aware observation simulation, and scalable egocentric video data construction.

references.title: EVA: Aligning Video World Models with Executable Robot Actions via Inverse Dynamics Rewards
references.notes: Support reference for IDM: generated video rollouts can be decoded into actions by an inverse dynamics model, and IDM-derived rewards can align video generation with executable robot constraints.

references.title: RoboEngine: Plug-and-Play Robot Data Augmentation with Semantic Robot Segmentation and Background Generation
references.notes: Support reference for scene layer: robot/object masks plus physics- and task-aware background generation for robot data augmentation and domain adaptation.

references.title: Single-View Robot Pose and Joint Angle Estimation via Render & Compare
references.notes: Support reference for visible-robot branch: RoboPose estimates robot joint angles and 6D camera-to-robot pose from a single RGB image of a known articulated robot.

references.title: ORB-SLAM3: An Accurate Open-Source Library for Visual, Visual-Inertial and Multi-Map SLAM
references.notes: Support reference for no-arm-visible branch: visual/visual-inertial SLAM supports monocular, stereo, RGB-D, pinhole, and fisheye cameras, suitable as a VO/SLAM baseline for wrist/head ego views.

references.title: VGGT: Visual Geometry Grounded Transformer
references.notes: Support reference for feed-forward visual geometry: predicts camera parameters, depth, point maps, and 3D point tracks from one or many views, useful for wrist/head egomotion and object-scene geometry.

references.title: SANA-WM: Efficient Minute-Scale World Modeling with Hybrid Linear Diffusion Transformer
references.notes: Reference for efficient minute-scale world modeling, hybrid linear attention, precise 6-DoF camera control, and metric pose-supervised long-video generation.

references.title: iWorld-Bench: A Benchmark for Interactive World Models with a Unified Action Generation Framework
references.notes: Reference for evaluating interactive/camera-controllable world models with packaged metadata, camera trajectories, action-control, memory-ability, camera-following, and all-metric evaluation wrappers.

references.title: EgoX: Egocentric Video Generation from a Single Exocentric Video
references.notes: Exo-to-ego reference: point-cloud rendered ego prior, width/channel latent concatenation, geometry-guided self-attention, LoRA on Wan 2.1 14B; useful for cross-view conditioning design.

references.title: Intention-driven Ego-to-Exo Video Generation
references.notes: Ego-to-exo reference: uses view-independent intention, head trajectory, action description, optical flow and occlusion maps to synthesize exocentric video from egocentric input.

references.title: WorldWander: Bridging Egocentric and Exocentric Worlds in Video Generation
references.notes: Bidirectional ego/exo translation reference with in-context perspective alignment and synchronized ego-exo triplets; relevant for tri-view cross-view synchronization.

references.title: MolmoAct: Action Reasoning Models that can Reason in Space
references.notes: Dataset-design reference for this project, not a direct substitute for our missing view: MolmoAct's real-robot dataset uses two side-mounted views and one wrist camera, converts episodes into side+wrist paired examples, and adds depth/trace/action-reasoning supervision.

references.title: MolmoAct2-BimanualYAM Dataset
references.notes: LeRobot-format schema reference for synchronized bimanual manipulation data: camera streams are observation.images.top / left / right at 360x640 30fps, with 14D bimanual state/action. Useful for dataset packaging and ablations, but it does not resolve the current UMI/Jianzhi missing fixed robot-ego view.

risks_decisions: Story rewrite 2026-06-09: project is now a two-stage Ego World + IDM foundation-model story, not just tri-view video generation or image-layer decomposition.
Ego-view decision: wrist cameras and fixed/floating robot-head cameras count as ego views. A fixed robot-head camera can be the third view even if it is not a detached exocentric lab camera.

Shared-context requirement: all views must share the same scene/object/action context; if POV overlap and synchronization are weak, the model becomes independent video generation rather than an interactive world model.
Action-conditioning requirement: the Ego World must accept real-time external action conditions. Without action responsiveness, it cannot support teleoperation-like interaction or IDM training.
IDM executability risk: visually plausible generated video is not enough. The IDM/control decoder must produce smooth and feasible position, velocity, or current/torque-loop commands.

Robot visibility branch: if the arm is visible, prioritize robot pose/joint estimation from the robot layer; if not visible, use VO/SLAM/visual geometry to estimate camera egomotion and infer actions from object-scene transitions.
Object-layer decision: object interaction state is the main process-reward target. ObjectForesight-like 6-DoF object trajectories are a better analogy than generic object masks alone.
Scene-layer decision: scene/background replacement is for physics/task-aware augmentation and domain adaptation, not for changing the core object-robot interaction label.

Data risk: layered video data is the main bottleneck. Traditional segmentation may not track well enough, so Qwen-Image-Layered, segment+inpaint, pure video generation prompts, and SVG/structured representations should all be tested on small samples first.
Gamma-World boundary: reproduce or baseline against Gamma-World, but claim novelty around ego-view interaction, object-interaction-robot layers, and IDM/control decoding.

timeline.target_note: Paper target for the tri-view layered manipulation world model; schedule should be updated once the exact submission plan is fixed.

milestones.title: Merge Image-Layered WM into UMI tri-view layered world-model card
Tri-view data schema and layer/eval ablation spec ready
